reasoning: The 5-year TIPS yield (DFII5) fell precipitously in late February 2025, reaching 1.45% on February 28, representing the lowest reading in the past 12 months. This sharp late-month drop placed the metric significantly below its short-term linear trendline, indicating a potential near-term oversold condition. In the short run (March 9 and April 1), financial metrics with such steep short-term deviations tend to experience a reversion or "bounce," which aligns with the FRED statistical model projecting a 0.64 probability of an increase back towards the ~1.48 trendline. In the medium term (May and August), broad mean reversion supports a move back toward the 2024 range of 1.7%-2.1%, though structural shifts to a lower-yield regime introduce widening confidence intervals. Per the forecasting rules, uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, pulling probabilities steadily closer to 0.50 (maximum entropy) for the longest-dated timelines (March 2026). Thus, probabilities cascade down from 0.64 in the near-term to 0.52 at the 1-year mark.
